satire_attempt: Wisdom for the Modern IC: every cool project that lands in your Jira queue is a Gateway To Heaven. Stop questioning. The Universe sent you the auth-rewrite. Stop asking whether the company actually needs it. The Sanskrit said yadṛcchayā so you have to take it. (Available now: my course on Receiving Your Cosmic Backlog Item.)
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to bless every unsolicited opportunity, but the falsifiability paragraph explicitly excludes this: 'A reorg into a VP role I never wanted but it landed in my inbox is not the verse. A cool greenfield rebuild that fits my résumé but the company does not actually need is not the verse.' The yadṛcchayā framing is necessary but not sufficient. The counter_example handles the 'opportunity-born-of-burnout' case. The satire would have to invert the scoping conditions to land. PASS.
